free_text: Thin target cross sections for proton-induced
production of radionuclides from lead for E(P)<71 MeV.
Department of nuclear chemistry
Center for radiation protection and
radioecology
Last checking has been done.
Two stacks consisting of high purity lead foils in
natural isotopic composition (99.9%) separated by high
purity aluminium and copper foils for flux monitoring
were used as targets.
.Model calculations on the basis of an improved
hybrid model of preequilibrium reactions.
Injector 1 cyclotron.
The stacks were irradiated for about 8 hours
at initial proton energies of 70.5 MeV and 45 MeV.
After irradiation each foil was subsequently measured
up to 15 times covering decay times from hours up to 6
months after irradiation.
All known error sources is systematical uncertainties
to be less than 8% not including the uncertainty due to
counting statistics.
.The uncertainty is reported by authors in
table.
.The uncertainty is reported by authors.
